extravagant waste or illegal despo-
liation.'


The Indians are not to be neg
lected by the new administration.


For years to come" said Bal
linger in response to a question,
''the government must continue to
exercise a supervisory control over
the tribal Indians. The government
should undertake not only to pro
tect them against combinations
seeking to divest them of their
property, but should also exercise
general guardianship over their
property and personal interests
wherever and whenever neces-
sary."


There have been complaints
from the north that Alaska has
been neglected" was suggested to
Judge Ballinger.


Alaska" was his reply, lis just
beginning to be recognized as the
greatest field still remaining in
American territory to be exploited.
1t is big country and wonderfully
rich and will undoubtedly figure
largely in the affairs of the new
administration.
